Comparte reflexiones sobre el algoritmo de Instagram y cómo el contenido generado por IA actúa como un impulso para la autenticidad en la plataforma. El episodio profundiza en desafíos y lecciones durante su liderazgo, incluyendo reflexiones sobre fracasos de producto, ofreciendo valiosas perspectivas sobre desarrollo de producto e influencia de la IA en redes sociales e identidad del creador.

title.original: Travel Through the Lens of AI with with Booking.com CEO Glenn Fogel
title.en: Travel Through the Lens of AI with with Booking.com CEO Glenn Fogel
title.zh: 通过人工智能视角探索旅行，访Booking.com CEO Glenn Fogel
title.es: Viaja a través del lente de la IA con el CEO de Booking.com, Glenn Fogel

summary.original: Glenn Fogel, CEO of Booking Holdings, is interviewed about his career, which spans from joining Priceline in 2000 to scaling Booking Holdings into a global travel giant valued at over $100 billion. The discussion touches on his background from law school and Wall Street and explores insights related to leading a major travel company. The episode likely covers themes of business growth and leadership in the travel sector.
summary.en: Glenn Fogel, CEO of Booking Holdings, is interviewed about his career, which spans from joining Priceline in 2000 to scaling Booking Holdings into a global travel giant valued at over $100 billion. The discussion touches on his background from law school and Wall Street and explores insights related to leading a major travel company. The episode likely covers themes of business growth and leadership in the travel sector.
summary.zh: 本期采访了Booking Holdings CEO Glenn Fogel，回顾他自2000年加入Priceline以来的职业历程，将Booking Holdings发展成为市值逾1000亿美元的全球旅游巨头。讨论涉及他的法学院背景及华尔街经历，探讨领导大型旅游公司的见解。这一集主题涵盖商业增长及旅游行业领导力。
summary.es: Glenn Fogel, CEO de Booking Holdings, es entrevistado sobre su carrera, que va desde unirse a Priceline en 2000 hasta escalar Booking Holdings a un gigante global de viajes valorado en más de 100 mil millones de dólares. La conversación toca sus antecedentes en derecho y Wall Street y ofrece perspectivas sobre liderar una gran empresa de viajes. El episodio aborda temas de crecimiento empresarial y liderazgo en el sector turístico.

title.original: Essentials: The Science & Treatment of Obsessive Compulsive Disorder (OCD)
title.en: Essentials: The Science & Treatment of Obsessive Compulsive Disorder (OCD)
title.zh: 必听：强迫症（OCD）的科学与治疗
title.es: Esenciales: La ciencia y tratamiento del Trastorno Obsesivo Compulsivo (TOC)

summary.original: This Huberman Lab Essentials episode focuses on the biology and psychology of obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD), explaining the neural circuitry behind repetitive thought-action loops. It discusses why compulsive actions can strengthen obsessions instead of relieving them. The episode covers effective treatments for OCD such as exposure-based cognitive behavioral therapy and SSRIs, including how they compare alone and combined. It also describes a clinical protocol where patients learn to suppress compulsive responses by entering anxiety states, aiming to retrain the brain and break the OCD cycle. The episode includes insights from Dr. Helen Blair Simpson, an expert on OCD.
summary.en: This Huberman Lab Essentials episode focuses on the biology and psychology of obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD), explaining the neural circuitry behind repetitive thought-action loops. It discusses why compulsive actions can strengthen obsessions instead of relieving them. The episode covers effective treatments for OCD such as exposure-based cognitive behavioral therapy and SSRIs, including how they compare alone and combined. It also describes a clinical protocol where patients learn to suppress compulsive responses by entering anxiety states, aiming to retrain the brain and break the OCD cycle. The episode includes insights from Dr. Helen Blair Simpson, an expert on OCD.
summary.zh: Huberman Lab必听节目聚焦强迫症的生物学和心理学，解释神经回路如何导致重复的思维和行为循环。讨论强迫行为为何加剧执念而非缓解症状。介绍了暴露认知行为疗法和选择性5-羟色胺再摄取抑制剂（SSRIs）等有效治疗方法及其单独和联合应用。描述临床方案中患者通过进入焦虑状态抑制强迫反应，重新训练大脑打破强迫循环。本集包含OCD专家Helen Blair Simpson博士的见解。
summary.es: Este episodio esencial de Huberman Lab se centra en la biología y psicología del trastorno obsesivo-compulsivo (TOC), explicando la circuitería neuronal detrás de los ciclos repetitivos de pensamiento y acción. Discute por qué las compulsiones pueden reforzar las obsesiones en lugar de aliviarlas. Cubre tratamientos efectivos como la terapia cognitivo-conductual basada en la exposición y los ISRS, incluyendo comparaciones de uso individual y combinado. Describe un protocolo clínico donde los pacientes aprenden a suprimir respuestas compulsivas entrando en estados de ansiedad, buscando reentrenar el cerebro y romper el ciclo del TOC. Incluye perspectivas de la experta en TOC, la Dra. Helen Blair Simpson.

title.original: He Loves AI. He Won't Let It Near His Books.
title.en: He Loves AI. He Won't Let It Near His Books.
title.zh: 他爱人工智能，但不让它接触他的书籍。
title.es: Él ama la IA. Pero no la deja tocar sus libros.

summary.original: This episode from AI & I discusses Craig Mod's experience with AI in newsletter management. He used to pay Campaign Monitor about $7,000 annually to send his newsletters but rebuilt the tool himself using AI, significantly cutting his costs to around $150. The episode explores how a writer can employ AI effectively without losing their unique voice, making it relevant for those interested in AI's practical applications in creative and professional workflows.
summary.en: This episode from AI & I discusses Craig Mod's experience with AI in newsletter management. He used to pay Campaign Monitor about $7,000 annually to send his newsletters but rebuilt the tool himself using AI, significantly cutting his costs to around $150. The episode explores how a writer can employ AI effectively without losing their unique voice, making it relevant for those interested in AI's practical applications in creative and professional workflows.
summary.zh: AI & I播客讲述Craig Mod利用人工智能重建新闻通讯工具，将年支出从约7000美元降至150美元。探讨了作家如何有效利用AI且保持独特声音，适合关注AI在创作和专业工作流程实践应用的听众。
summary.es: Este episodio de AI & I aborda la experiencia de Craig Mod con la IA en la gestión de newsletters. Antes pagaba unos 7,000 dólares anuales y ahora ha reconstruido la herramienta usando IA, reduciendo costos a 150 dólares. Explora cómo un escritor puede usar IA sin perder su voz única.

title.original: The AI Infra Stack: DeFlash, Auto Endpoints, RDMA, and Sandboxes — Akshat Bubna, Modal CTO
title.en: The AI Infra Stack: DeFlash, Auto Endpoints, RDMA, and Sandboxes — Akshat Bubna, Modal CTO
title.zh: AI基础设施堆栈：DeFlash，自动端点，RDMA与沙箱——Modal首席技术官Akshat Bubna
title.es: La pila de infraestructura de IA: DeFlash, Auto Endpoints, RDMA y Sandboxes — Akshat Bubna, CTO de Modal

summary.original: In this Latent Space episode, Akshat Bubna, Modal CTO, talks about the AI infrastructure stack, covering topics like DeFlash, Auto Endpoints, RDMA, and agent sandboxes. The discussion includes insights on innovations such as adding GPUs before ChatGPT and developing cloud primitives for elastic inference, post-training, and production AI workloads. The episode is valuable for listeners interested in the technical and infrastructural aspects behind AI systems.
summary.en: In this Latent Space episode, Akshat Bubna, Modal CTO, talks about the AI infrastructure stack, covering topics like DeFlash, Auto Endpoints, RDMA, and agent sandboxes. The discussion includes insights on innovations such as adding GPUs before ChatGPT and developing cloud primitives for elastic inference, post-training, and production AI workloads. The episode is valuable for listeners interested in the technical and infrastructural aspects behind AI systems.
summary.zh: Latent Space访谈Modal首席技术官Akshat Bubna，讨论AI基础设施相关技术，如DeFlash、自动端点、RDMA和代理沙箱，以及云原语等创新。适合关注AI系统技术架构的听众。
summary.es: En este episodio de Latent Space, Akshat Bubna, CTO de Modal, habla sobre la pila de infraestructura de IA cubriendo temas como DeFlash, Auto Endpoints, RDMA y sandboxes. Incluye innovaciones previas a ChatGPT y desarrollo de primitivas cloud para inferencia elástica y cargas AI en producción.

title.original: Why AI Infrastructure must evolve for Agent Experience — Akshat Bubna, Modal CTO
title.en: Why AI Infrastructure must evolve for Agent Experience — Akshat Bubna, Modal CTO
title.zh: 为何AI基础设施必须为代理体验进化 — Akshat Bubna，Modal首席技术官
title.es: Por qué la infraestructura de IA debe evolucionar para la experiencia de agentes — Akshat Bubna, CTO de Modal

summary.original: In this Latent Space podcast episode, Modal CTO Akshat Bubna discusses why AI infrastructure must evolve to support agent experiences. The episode covers Modal's growth and its focus shift from developer experience to agent experience, emphasizing challenges with traditional cloud infrastructure in handling bursty, compute-heavy AI workloads. Topics include the need for sandboxes, elastic inference, GPU snapshotting, and other innovations enabling AI agents to operate efficiently. The discussion also touches on Modal's strategy across multiple cloud providers and the technological complexity behind modern AI infrastructure.
summary.en: In this Latent Space podcast episode, Modal CTO Akshat Bubna discusses why AI infrastructure must evolve to support agent experiences. The episode covers Modal's growth and its focus shift from developer experience to agent experience, emphasizing challenges with traditional cloud infrastructure in handling bursty, compute-heavy AI workloads. Topics include the need for sandboxes, elastic inference, GPU snapshotting, and other innovations enabling AI agents to operate efficiently. The discussion also touches on Modal's strategy across multiple cloud providers and the technological complexity behind modern AI infrastructure.
summary.zh: 本期Latent Space播客中，Modal CTO Akshat Bubna探讨AI基础设施为何需要进化以支持代理体验。内容涵盖Modal的成长及其重点从开发者体验转向代理体验，强调传统云基础设施在处理突发且计算密集的AI工作负载时的挑战，及沙箱、弹性推理、GPU快照等创新。还讨论了Modal在多云环境下的战略及现代AI基础设施背后的技术复杂性。
summary.es: En este episodio del podcast Latent Space, Akshat Bubna, CTO de Modal, explica por qué la infraestructura de IA debe evolucionar para apoyar la experiencia de agentes. Se aborda el crecimiento de Modal y su cambio de enfoque de la experiencia del desarrollador a la del agente, destacando los retos de la infraestructura cloud tradicional ante cargas de trabajo de IA intensivas y erráticas, así como innovaciones como sandboxes, inferencia elástica y snapshots de GPU. También se discuten las estrategias de Modal en múltiples proveedores cloud y la complejidad tecnológica detrás de la infraestructura moderna de IA.

title.original: Inside Zipline's Autonomous System: 140M Miles, Zero Incidents
title.en: Inside Zipline's Autonomous System: 140M Miles, Zero Incidents
title.zh: Zipline自动系统揭秘：1.4亿英里，零事故
title.es: Dentro del sistema autónomo de Zipline: 140 millones de millas, cero incidentes

summary.original: Training Data's episode focuses on Zipline's autonomous system, which has flown 140 million autonomous miles with zero safety incidents. It highlights Zipline as the largest commercial autonomous system on Earth, surpassing robotaxi fleets in scale. This episode offers insight into real-world autonomous system deployment and safety performance, making it worthwhile for those interested in AI-powered robotics and autonomous transportation systems.
summary.en: Training Data's episode focuses on Zipline's autonomous system, which has flown 140 million autonomous miles with zero safety incidents. It highlights Zipline as the largest commercial autonomous system on Earth, surpassing robotaxi fleets in scale. This episode offers insight into real-world autonomous system deployment and safety performance, making it worthwhile for those interested in AI-powered robotics and autonomous transportation systems.
summary.zh: Training Data聚焦地球上最大商业自动系统Zipline，其自动飞行超过1.4亿英里且无安全事故。适合关注AI驱动机器人和自动运输系统部署与安全表现的听众。
summary.es: El episodio de Training Data se centra en el sistema autónomo de Zipline, que ha volado 140 millones de millas sin incidentes de seguridad. Destaca que Zipline es el mayor sistema autónomo comercial, superando flotas robotaxi. Ideal para interesados en robótica y transporte autónomo con IA.

title.original: Raising a Dog & Mastering Calm Assertive Energy | Cesar Millan
title.en: Raising a Dog & Mastering Calm Assertive Energy | Cesar Millan
title.zh: 养狗与掌握冷静自信的能量 | Cesar Millan
title.es: Criar un perro y dominar una energía tranquila y asertiva | Cesar Millan

summary.original: In this Huberman Lab episode, dog behavior expert Cesar Millan discusses how to adjust human energy and behavior to ensure dogs remain calm, happy, and responsive. Cesar shares tools for structuring daily dog interactions such as greetings, walks, and reducing aggression and anxiety. The episode explores concepts like pack order in dogs and human energy dynamics, providing insights valuable to both dog owners and others. It also covers topics including maintaining calm confidence, managing destructive behaviors, and fostering strong human-dog relationships.
summary.en: In this Huberman Lab episode, dog behavior expert Cesar Millan discusses how to adjust human energy and behavior to ensure dogs remain calm, happy, and responsive. Cesar shares tools for structuring daily dog interactions such as greetings, walks, and reducing aggression and anxiety. The episode explores concepts like pack order in dogs and human energy dynamics, providing insights valuable to both dog owners and others. It also covers topics including maintaining calm confidence, managing destructive behaviors, and fostering strong human-dog relationships.
summary.zh: 本期Huberman Lab中，犬类行为专家Cesar Millan讲述如何调整人类能量和行为，确保狗保持冷静、快乐和顺从。分享了结构化日常互动的工具，如问候、散步及减少攻击与焦虑。探讨犬群秩序和人类能量动力学，对狗主人及相关人群有价值。内容还涵盖保持冷静自信、管理破坏行为及增进人犬关系。
summary.es: En este episodio de Huberman Lab, el experto en comportamiento canino Cesar Millan discute cómo ajustar la energía y comportamiento humanos para que los perros se mantengan calmados, felices y receptivos. Comparte herramientas para estructurar las interacciones diarias como saludos, paseos y la reducción de agresión y ansiedad. Explora conceptos como el orden en la manada y las dinámicas de energía humana, proporcionando insights útiles para dueños de perros y otros. También aborda cómo mantener la calma con confianza, manejar conductas destructivas y fortalecer la relación humano-perro.

title.original: Building strength and muscle mass: how to optimize training, nutrition, and more for longevity (AMA #71 rebroadcast)
title.en: Building strength and muscle mass: how to optimize training, nutrition, and more for longevity (AMA #71 rebroadcast)
title.zh: 打造力量与肌肉：如何优化训练、营养及更多以延长寿命（AMA#71重播）
title.es: Construcción de fuerza y masa muscular: cómo optimizar entrenamiento, nutrición y más para la longevidad (Repetición AMA #71)

summary.original: This "Ask Me Anything" episode of The Peter Attia Drive focuses on building muscle mass and strength, emphasizing their roles in lifespan and healthspan. Peter Attia synthesizes insights from over 30 hours of prior discussions with experts such as Layne Norton, Andy Galpin, and Mike Israetel. The episode covers why muscle matters for longevity, metabolic health, injury prevention, and clarifies distinctions between muscle mass and strength. It provides practical training strategies, nutrition guidance including protein timing and creatine supplementation, and advice tailored for beginners and experienced lifters alike, aiming to promote safe and sustainable muscle development and maintenance.
summary.en: This "Ask Me Anything" episode of The Peter Attia Drive focuses on building muscle mass and strength, emphasizing their roles in lifespan and healthspan. Peter Attia synthesizes insights from over 30 hours of prior discussions with experts such as Layne Norton, Andy Galpin, and Mike Israetel. The episode covers why muscle matters for longevity, metabolic health, injury prevention, and clarifies distinctions between muscle mass and strength. It provides practical training strategies, nutrition guidance including protein timing and creatine supplementation, and advice tailored for beginners and experienced lifters alike, aiming to promote safe and sustainable muscle development and maintenance.
summary.zh: Peter Attia Drive的“问我任何事”节目聚焦肌肉质量与力量的建立，强调其在寿命与健康寿命中的重要性。Peter汇总了与多位专家的30小时讨论内容，介绍肌肉对长寿、新陈代谢健康和预防伤害的作用，区分肌肉质量与力量。提供实用训练策略、营养建议（包括蛋白质时机和肌酸补充），帮助初学者和有经验者安全且持续地增肌维持。
summary.es: Este episodio "Ask Me Anything" de The Peter Attia Drive se centra en construir masa muscular y fuerza, destacando su rol en la longevidad y salud. Peter Attia sintetiza más de 30 horas de charlas con expertos como Layne Norton, Andy Galpin y Mike Israetel. Explica por qué el músculo es vital para la longevidad, salud metabólica y prevención de lesiones, y aclara diferencias entre masa muscular y fuerza. Proporciona estrategias prácticas de entrenamiento, consejos nutricionales (incluyendo el tiempo de proteínas y suplementos de creatina), dirigidos a principiantes y levantadores avanzados para un desarrollo seguro y sostenible.

title.original: How Nuclear Will Unlock Energy Abundance with Valar Atomics Founder Isaiah Taylor
title.en: How Nuclear Will Unlock Energy Abundance with Valar Atomics Founder Isaiah Taylor
title.zh: 核能如何释放能源丰裕：Valar Atomics创始人Isaiah Taylor谈核能潜力
title.es: Cómo la energía nuclear desbloqueará la abundancia energética con Isaiah Taylor, fundador de Valar Atomics

summary.original: In this No Priors YouTube episode, the founder of Valar Atomics, Isaiah Taylor, discusses nuclear energy's potential to unlock energy abundance. The episode highlights Valar Atomics' advanced approach, including powering an NVIDIA Blackwell chip directly with a live nuclear reactor, illustrating practical nuclear applications. The conversation offers insights into innovations in nuclear technology and its role in shifting energy paradigms toward abundance.
summary.en: In this No Priors YouTube episode, the founder of Valar Atomics, Isaiah Taylor, discusses nuclear energy's potential to unlock energy abundance. The episode highlights Valar Atomics' advanced approach, including powering an NVIDIA Blackwell chip directly with a live nuclear reactor, illustrating practical nuclear applications. The conversation offers insights into innovations in nuclear technology and its role in shifting energy paradigms toward abundance.
summary.zh: 在本期No Priors的YouTube节目中，Valar Atomics创始人Isaiah Taylor讨论了核能释放能源丰裕的潜力。节目突出Valar Atomics的先进方法，包括用活核反应堆直接为NVIDIA Blackwell芯片供电，展示了核能的实际应用。讨论提供了核技术创新及其推动能源丰裕转变的见解。
summary.es: En este episodio de No Priors en YouTube, Isaiah Taylor, fundador de Valar Atomics, habla sobre el potencial de la energía nuclear para desbloquear la abundancia energética. El episodio destaca el enfoque avanzado de Valar Atomics, que incluye alimentar directamente un chip NVIDIA Blackwell con un reactor nuclear en vivo, ilustrando aplicaciones prácticas de la energía nuclear. La conversación ofrece ideas sobre innovaciones en tecnología nuclear y su papel en cambiar paradigmas energéticos hacia la abundancia.

title.original: How Nuclear Will Unlock Energy Abundance with Valar Atomics Founder Isaiah Taylor
title.en: How Nuclear Will Unlock Energy Abundance with Valar Atomics Founder Isaiah Taylor
title.zh: 核能如何释放能源丰裕：Valar Atomics创始人Isaiah Taylor谈核能潜力
title.es: Cómo la energía nuclear desbloqueará la abundancia energética con Isaiah Taylor, fundador de Valar Atomics

summary.original: This No Priors podcast episode features Isaiah Taylor, founder and CEO of Valar Atomics, discussing how their company is advancing nuclear technology to unlock energy abundance. Unlike much of the nuclear industry relying on simulations and designs, Valar Atomics operates an active nuclear reactor used to power digital devices, exemplified by powering an NVIDIA Blackwell chip and the world's first nuclear-powered website. The episode includes Sarah Guo joining Taylor on-site at their Utah reactor, offering insight into practical nuclear energy deployment and its potential influence on energy infrastructure.
summary.en: This No Priors podcast episode features Isaiah Taylor, founder and CEO of Valar Atomics, discussing how their company is advancing nuclear technology to unlock energy abundance. Unlike much of the nuclear industry relying on simulations and designs, Valar Atomics operates an active nuclear reactor used to power digital devices, exemplified by powering an NVIDIA Blackwell chip and the world's first nuclear-powered website. The episode includes Sarah Guo joining Taylor on-site at their Utah reactor, offering insight into practical nuclear energy deployment and its potential influence on energy infrastructure.
summary.zh: 本期No Priors播客邀请Valar Atomics创始人兼CEO Isaiah Taylor，讨论他们如何通过先进核技术实现能源丰裕。与依赖模拟设计的核能行业不同，Valar Atomics运营活核反应堆，实测为数字设备供电，如NVIDIA Blackwell芯片及首个核能驱动网站。Sarah Guo现场参与，详解实际核能部署及其对能源基础设施的潜在影响。
summary.es: En este episodio del podcast No Priors, Isaiah Taylor, fundador y CEO de Valar Atomics, habla sobre cómo su empresa está avanzando en tecnología nuclear para desbloquear la abundancia energética. A diferencia de gran parte de la industria nuclear que depende de simulaciones y diseños, Valar Atomics opera un reactor nuclear activo para alimentar dispositivos digitales, ejemplificado por un chip NVIDIA Blackwell y el primer sitio web impulsado por energía nuclear. Sarah Guo se une a Taylor en su reactor en Utah, ofreciendo perspectivas sobre el despliegue práctico de energía nuclear y su posible influencia en la infraestructura energética.

title.original: Essentials: Tools for Hormone Optimization in Males | Dr. Kyle Gillett
title.en: Essentials: Tools for Hormone Optimization in Males | Dr. Kyle Gillett
title.zh: 必备：男性激素优化工具 | Dr. Kyle Gillett
title.es: Esenciales: Herramientas para la Optimización Hormonal en Hombres | Dr. Kyle Gillett

summary.original: In this Huberman Lab Essentials episode, Dr. Kyle Gillett, a dual board-certified expert in family medicine and obesity medicine specializing in hormone optimization, discusses science-based tools for optimizing male hormones through life. The episode covers the role of blood tests, nutrition, exercise, testosterone therapy, and supplements like creatine, L-carnitine, Tongkat Ali, and Fadogia agrestis. Topics also include managing hair loss and prostate health. This concise science-driven discussion provides practical hormone health insights for males across different ages.
summary.en: In this Huberman Lab Essentials episode, Dr. Kyle Gillett, a dual board-certified expert in family medicine and obesity medicine specializing in hormone optimization, discusses science-based tools for optimizing male hormones through life. The episode covers the role of blood tests, nutrition, exercise, testosterone therapy, and supplements like creatine, L-carnitine, Tongkat Ali, and Fadogia agrestis. Topics also include managing hair loss and prostate health. This concise science-driven discussion provides practical hormone health insights for males across different ages.
summary.zh: 本期Huberman Lab Essentials节目中，双重认证的家庭医学和肥胖医学专家Dr. Kyle Gillett讲解通过科学方法优化男性激素。内容涵盖血液检测、营养、锻炼、睾酮疗法及补充剂（肌酸、L-肉碱、东革阿里、Fadogia agrestis），并讨论脱发管理与前列腺健康，提供适合不同年龄男性的实用激素健康建议。
summary.es: En este episodio de Huberman Lab Essentials, el Dr. Kyle Gillett, experto certificado en medicina familiar y obesidad especializado en optimización hormonal, discute herramientas científicas para optimizar las hormonas masculinas a lo largo de la vida. El episodio cubre análisis de sangre, nutrición, ejercicio, terapia de testosterona y suplementos como creatina, L-carnitina, Tongkat Ali y Fadogia agrestis. También aborda la gestión de la pérdida de cabello y la salud prostática. Una charla breve y basada en ciencia con consejos prácticos para la salud hormonal masculina en todas las edades.

title.original: The AI Workflows Behind Every's Consulting Team
title.en: The AI Workflows Behind Every's Consulting Team
title.zh: Every咨询团队背后的人工智能工作流程
title.es: Los flujos de trabajo de IA detrás del equipo de consultoría de Every

summary.original: This AI & I episode features Natalia Quintero, head of consulting at Every, who is integrating AI into workflows for executives at hedge funds, private equity firms, and tech companies. The discussion centers on how Every's consulting team leverages AI tools like Codex in daily operations. It provides a perspective on applying AI in business consulting, valuable for professionals curious about AI's impact on executive-level decision-making and workflow optimization.
summary.en: This AI & I episode features Natalia Quintero, head of consulting at Every, who is integrating AI into workflows for executives at hedge funds, private equity firms, and tech companies. The discussion centers on how Every's consulting team leverages AI tools like Codex in daily operations. It provides a perspective on applying AI in business consulting, valuable for professionals curious about AI's impact on executive-level decision-making and workflow optimization.

summary.es: Este episodio de AI & I presenta a Natalia Quintero, jefa de consultoría en Every, quien integra IA en flujos de trabajo para ejecutivos de hedge funds, private equity y empresas tech. Habla sobre el uso de herramientas IA como Codex en operaciones diarias, útil para profesionales interesados en IA aplicada a consultoría de alto nivel.

title.original: 🔬 The Coolest Diffusion Research Isn't in LLMs — Evan Feinberg & Sergey Edunov, Genesis Molecular AI
title.en: 🔬 The Coolest Diffusion Research Isn't in LLMs — Evan Feinberg & Sergey Edunov, Genesis Molecular AI
title.zh: 🔬 最酷的扩散研究不在大型语言模型 — Evan Feinberg与Sergey Edunov，Genesis Molecular AI
title.es: 🔬 La investigación de difusión más innovadora no está en los LLMs — Evan Feinberg y Sergey Edunov, Genesis Molecular AI

summary.original: This Latent Space Podcast episode features Evan Feinberg and Sergey Edunov from Genesis Molecular AI discussing how diffusion research in drug discovery is leading innovation beyond language models. Sergey, previously leading Llama training at Meta, shares insights on the novel architectures developed at Genesis. They highlight how their flagship model, PEARL, advances 3D protein-ligand structure prediction by modeling protein flexibility, improving drug binding predictions. The episode explores challenges in small molecule drug discovery, agentic AI workflows for iterative drug design, and critiques current academic benchmarks. It’s valuable for those interested in AI’s impact on molecular drug discovery and breakthroughs enabling practical real-world applications.
summary.en: This Latent Space Podcast episode features Evan Feinberg and Sergey Edunov from Genesis Molecular AI discussing how diffusion research in drug discovery is leading innovation beyond language models. Sergey, previously leading Llama training at Meta, shares insights on the novel architectures developed at Genesis. They highlight how their flagship model, PEARL, advances 3D protein-ligand structure prediction by modeling protein flexibility, improving drug binding predictions. The episode explores challenges in small molecule drug discovery, agentic AI workflows for iterative drug design, and critiques current academic benchmarks. It’s valuable for those interested in AI’s impact on molecular drug discovery and breakthroughs enabling practical real-world applications.
summary.zh: 本期Latent Space播客邀请Genesis Molecular AI的Evan Feinberg与Sergey Edunov，探讨扩散技术在药物发现领域的创新，超越语言模型。Sergey曾主导Meta Llama训练，分享Genesis的新型架构。重点介绍旗舰模型PEARL如何通过模拟蛋白质柔性提升3D蛋白-配体结构预测和药物结合精度。节目涵盖小分子药物开发难题、迭代药物设计的智能AI流程及对现有学术基准的批评。对关注AI在分子药物研发实用突破者极具价值。
summary.es: En este episodio del podcast Latent Space, Evan Feinberg y Sergey Edunov de Genesis Molecular AI discuten cómo la investigación en difusión para el descubrimiento de fármacos está liderando la innovación más allá de los modelos de lenguaje. Sergey, exlíder del entrenamiento de Llama en Meta, comparte información sobre las nuevas arquitecturas desarrolladas en Genesis. Destacan cómo su modelo principal, PEARL, mejora la predicción de estructuras 3D de proteínas y ligandos modelando la flexibilidad proteica, mejorando la precisión de la unión farmacológica. El episodio explora desafíos en descubrimiento de fármacos de pequeñas moléculas, flujos de trabajo de IA para diseño iterativo de fármacos y críticas a los estándares académicos actuales. Es valioso para quienes interesan en el impacto de la IA en el descubrimiento molecular de fármacos y avances prácticos.

title.original: Why Hardware-Software Co-Design Is AI's Real 100x: Dylan Patel of SemiAnalysis

summary.original: In this Training Data podcast episode, Dylan Patel, founder of SemiAnalysis, discusses the significance of hardware-software co-design in AI development. He argues that the most substantial improvements in AI come not just from faster chips but from optimizing interactions between hardware and software. This conversation can offer valuable perspectives on how AI performance gains are achieved, making it relevant for listeners interested in AI advancements, hardware, software engineering, and developer insights.

He argues that the most substantial improvements in AI come not just from faster chips but from optimizing interactions between hardware and software. title.original: #498 – Anthony Kaldellis: Roman Empire, Byzantine Empire, Rise & Fall of Empires

summary.original: In Lex Fridman Podcast episode #498, historian Anthony Kaldellis discusses the Roman and Byzantine Empires. As author of "The New Roman Empire," Kaldellis provides a comprehensive look at Eastern Roman history. The episode covers over two millennia of Roman history, significant political and cultural events, and lessons from the empire's longevity and transformations. It offers detailed historical insights for listeners interested in the complexities of empire rise and fall as analyzed by a subject-matter expert.

As author of "The New Roman Empire," Kaldellis provides a comprehensive look at Eastern Roman history. summary.zh: Lex Fridman播客#498集中讲述历史学家安东尼·卡尔德利斯关于罗马及拜占庭帝国的深度对话，涵盖两千多年历史及重要政治文化事件，深入探讨帝国的持续与变革，为喜欢帝国兴衰复杂历史的听众提供专家视角。
summary.es: En el episodio #498 del Podcast de Lex Fridman, el historiador Anthony Kaldellis analiza los Imperios Romano y Bizantino, cubriendo más de dos milenios de historia, eventos políticos y culturales relevantes, y lecciones sobre la permanencia y transformaciones imperiales, ideal para quienes buscan análisis expertos sobre imperios.

This episode is valuable for those curious about AI's evolving impact on mathematics and conceptual breakthroughs.

title.es: Gran computación en tiempo de prueba en IA cambia benchmarks, seguridad e investigación con Noam Brown de OpenAI

summary.original: The No Priors YouTube episode titled "Really Big Test-Time Compute in AI Changes Benchmarks, Safety and Research with OpenAI's Noam Brown" discusses how traditional AI benchmarks, which are based on fixed evaluation grids, fail to account for the time an AI model is allowed to think. The episode features OpenAI research scientist Noam Brown who talks about the implications of large-scale test-time compute on AI benchmarking, safety, and research. This discussion is pertinent for those interested in understanding evolving methods to measure AI capabilities beyond static benchmarks.

summary.es: El episodio de No Priors con Noam Brown de OpenAI analiza cómo la computación masiva en tiempo de prueba cambia los benchmarks tradicionales, la seguridad y la investigación en IA, proponiendo nuevas formas de medir capacidades AI.

title.original: #398 ‒ AMA #86: GLP-1 RAs and muscle loss: new data, better questions, and how to preserve muscle during weight loss
title.en: #398 ‒ AMA #86: GLP-1 RAs and muscle loss: new data, better questions, and how to preserve muscle during weight loss
title.zh: #398 ‒ AMA #86：GLP-1受体激动剂与肌肉流失——新数据、更好问题及如何在减重时保护肌肉
title.es: #398 ‒ AMA #86: Agonistas GLP-1 y pérdida muscular: nuevos datos, mejores preguntas y cómo preservar el músculo durante la pérdida de peso

summary.original: In this Ask Me Anything episode (#398) of The Peter Attia Drive, Peter explores the effects of GLP-1 receptor agonists—including drugs like Ozempic, Wegovy, and Zepbound—particularly focusing on their impact on muscle. He examines lean mass loss during treatment, compares it to other weight-loss approaches, and explains why DEXA scans can misrepresent muscle loss. The episode also covers effects on bone health, fracture risk, and functional outcomes, practical strategies to preserve muscle and bone health during weight loss, and early insights into retatrutide. The episode offers a deep dive for listeners interested in the nuanced effects of these medications and ways to mitigate muscle loss.
summary.en: In this Ask Me Anything episode (#398) of The Peter Attia Drive, Peter explores the effects of GLP-1 receptor agonists—including drugs like Ozempic, Wegovy, and Zepbound—particularly focusing on their impact on muscle. He examines lean mass loss during treatment, compares it to other weight-loss approaches, and explains why DEXA scans can misrepresent muscle loss. The episode also covers effects on bone health, fracture risk, and functional outcomes, practical strategies to preserve muscle and bone health during weight loss, and early insights into retatrutide. The episode offers a deep dive for listeners interested in the nuanced effects of these medications and ways to mitigate muscle loss.
summary.zh: Peter Attia的AMA节目探讨GLP-1受体激动剂（如Ozempic、Wegovy、Zepbound）对肌肉和骨骼健康的影响，讨论肌肉流失误判和保护策略，以及早期retatrutide洞见。
summary.es: En este episodio AMA de The Peter Attia Drive, se abordan los efectos de los agonistas GLP-1 (como Ozempic, Wegovy y Zepbound) sobre la masa muscular, riesgos óseos, estrategias para preservar músculo y hueso durante la pérdida de peso y primeros datos sobre retatrutide.

The discussion revolves around how AI has transformed product development, the importance of 'taste' as a professional skill in an AI-first workplace, and insights into building the Codex desktop experience aimed to be the best desktop app. Andrew talks about team organization at OpenAI, the vision for integrating Codex with other tools like ChatGPT, and practical uses of Codex. The episode provides valuable perspectives on AI's impact on software creation and management, making it worthwhile for listeners interested in AI-driven product development.
summary.zh: 本期Lenny播客邀请OpenAI Codex桌面应用开发负责人Andrew Ambrosino，探讨AI如何变革产品开发、专业技能“品味”的重要性，以及Codex桌面体验的打造理念。Andrew分享了OpenAI的团队组织、Codex与ChatGPT等工具的整合愿景及实际应用，提供了关于AI驱动软件创造与管理的宝贵视角。
summary.es: Este episodio del podcast de Lenny presenta a Andrew Ambrosino, líder del desarrollo de la aplicación de escritorio Codex en OpenAI. La conversación trata sobre cómo la IA ha transformado el desarrollo de productos, la importancia del 'gusto' como habilidad profesional en un entorno de IA, y detalles sobre la experiencia de escritorio de Codex. Andrew habla de la organización del equipo en OpenAI, la visión de integrar Codex con otras herramientas como ChatGPT, y usos prácticos de Codex, ofreciendo perspectivas valiosas sobre el impacto de la IA en la creación y gestión de software.

title.original: The next big breakthrough will be AIs learning on the job
title.en: The next big breakthrough will be AIs learning on the job
title.zh: 下一重大突破将来自AI的在职学习
title.es: El próximo gran avance será el aprendizaje en el trabajo de las IA

summary.original: In this podcast episode from Dwarkesh, the discussion centers on the future of AI with the hypothesis that the next major breakthrough will come from AIs learning on the job. The episode critiques the current training paradigm that relies heavily on massive data from simulated environments and suggests this is insufficient for complex real-world skills. It explores challenges around sample efficiency, continual learning, and learning in dynamic environments such as politics or business, emphasizing that future AI advancement depends on models handling ambiguous, sparse real-world interactions effectively. This episode is significant for those interested in AI research directions and limitations of current approaches.
summary.en: In this podcast episode from Dwarkesh, the discussion centers on the future of AI with the hypothesis that the next major breakthrough will come from AIs learning on the job. The episode critiques the current training paradigm that relies heavily on massive data from simulated environments and suggests this is insufficient for complex real-world skills. It explores challenges around sample efficiency, continual learning, and learning in dynamic environments such as politics or business, emphasizing that future AI advancement depends on models handling ambiguous, sparse real-world interactions effectively. This episode is significant for those interested in AI research directions and limitations of current approaches.
summary.zh: 本期Dwarkesh播客讨论未来AI的发展，假设下一个重大突破将来自AI在实际工作中的学习。节目批评现有依赖模拟环境大规模数据的训练方式，指出其不足以应对复杂现实技能。讨论涵盖样本效率、持续学习和动态环境中的学习，强调未来AI需有效处理真实世界的模糊和稀疏交互。适合关注AI研究方向和当前方法局限的听众。
summary.es: En este episodio del podcast de Dwarkesh se analiza el futuro de la IA con la hipótesis de que el próximo gran avance provendrá del aprendizaje en el trabajo de las IA. El episodio critica el paradigma actual que depende en gran medida de grandes datos de entornos simulados y sugiere que esto es insuficiente para habilidades complejas del mundo real. Explora retos como la eficiencia de muestras, aprendizaje continuo y aprendizaje en entornos dinámicos, enfatizando que el avance futuro de la IA depende de modelos capaces de manejar interacciones reales ambiguas y escasas. Es relevante para interesados en direcciones de investigación en IA y limitaciones actuales.

title.original: Really Big Test-Time Compute in AI Changes Benchmarks, Safety and Research with OpenAI Research Scientist Noam Brown
title.en: Really Big Test-Time Compute in AI Changes Benchmarks, Safety and Research with OpenAI Research Scientist Noam Brown
title.zh: AI中超大测试计算改变基准、安全与研究——OpenAI科学家Noam Brown访谈
title.es: Cálculo masivo en tiempo de prueba en IA cambia benchmarks, seguridad e investigación con el científico de OpenAI Noam Brown

summary.original: This No Priors Podcast episode features OpenAI research scientist Noam Brown discussing issues with traditional AI benchmarks that use fixed evaluation grids not accounting for model thinking time. The conversation covers how incorporating large-scale test-time compute changes how AI models are evaluated, affecting assessments of capability, safety, and research directions. This episode offers insights into evolving AI evaluation methods and is relevant for listeners interested in AI model performance and benchmarking.
summary.en: This No Priors Podcast episode features OpenAI research scientist Noam Brown discussing issues with traditional AI benchmarks that use fixed evaluation grids not accounting for model thinking time. The conversation covers how incorporating large-scale test-time compute changes how AI models are evaluated, affecting assessments of capability, safety, and research directions. This episode offers insights into evolving AI evaluation methods and is relevant for listeners interested in AI model performance and benchmarking.
summary.zh: No Priors播客邀请OpenAI研究员Noam Brown，讨论传统AI基准测试使用固定评估网格未考虑模型思考时间的问题。谈及大规模测试时计算如何影响AI模型评估，进而影响能力、安全和研究方向评估。本期为关注AI性能和基准测试方法演变的听众提供深刻见解。
summary.es: Este episodio del podcast No Priors presenta al científico investigador de OpenAI Noam Brown discutiendo problemas con benchmarks tradicionales de IA que usan rejillas fijas sin considerar el tiempo de pensamiento del modelo. La conversación aborda cómo la incorporación de gran cálculo en tiempo de prueba cambia la evaluación de modelos de IA, afectando evaluaciones de capacidad, seguridad y direcciones de investigación. Ofrece perspectivas sobre la evolución de métodos de evaluación de IA, relevante para quienes siguen desempeño y benchmarking de modelos.

title.original: Why the Frontier Ecosystem must be Open — Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin, Databricks
title.en: Why the Frontier Ecosystem must be Open — Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin, Databricks
title.zh: 前沿生态必须开放——Databricks联合创始人Matei Zaharia与Reynold Xin
title.es: Por qué el ecosistema Frontier debe ser abierto — Matei Zaharia y Reynold Xin, Databricks

summary.original: The Latent Space Podcast features a rare double-interview with Databricks co-founders Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin discussing the evolution of Databricks from big data company to an operating system for enterprise AI agents. They cover open-source projects like Omnigent, addressing challenges around AI agent collaboration, security, and integration across platforms. Technical topics include Databricks' database innovations such as LTAP and Lakebase architecture, and the importance of delivering the right data context to AI at the right moment. The episode offers a comprehensive look into how Databricks is shaping agent ecosystems, making it valuable for listeners interested in AI infrastructure and enterprise AI innovation.
summary.en: The Latent Space Podcast features a rare double-interview with Databricks co-founders Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin discussing the evolution of Databricks from big data company to an operating system for enterprise AI agents. They cover open-source projects like Omnigent, addressing challenges around AI agent collaboration, security, and integration across platforms. Technical topics include Databricks' database innovations such as LTAP and Lakebase architecture, and the importance of delivering the right data context to AI at the right moment. The episode offers a comprehensive look into how Databricks is shaping agent ecosystems, making it valuable for listeners interested in AI infrastructure and enterprise AI innovation.
summary.zh: Latent Space播客罕见地采访Databricks联合创始人Matei Zaharia和Reynold Xin，谈论Databricks从大数据公司转型为企业AI代理操作系统。内容涵盖开源项目Omnigent，AI代理协作、安全与平台整合挑战，以及LTAP和Lakebase架构等数据库创新。强调提供实时恰当数据上下文的重要性。本期为关注AI基础设施与企业AI创新者提供全面视角。
summary.es: El podcast Latent Space presenta una doble entrevista con los cofundadores de Databricks, Matei Zaharia y Reynold Xin, discutiendo la evolución de Databricks de empresa de big data a sistema operativo para agentes IA empresariales. Cubren proyectos open source como Omnigent, desafíos en colaboración, seguridad e integración de agentes IA en plataformas, innovaciones en base de datos como LTAP y arquitectura Lakebase, y la importancia de entregar el contexto adecuado a la IA en el momento justo. Ofrece una visión completa para interesados en infraestructura y innovación IA empresarial.

title.original: The data black hole at the center of AI
title.en: The data black hole at the center of AI
title.zh: AI中心的数据黑洞
title.es: El agujero negro de datos en el centro de la IA

summary.original: The YouTube video titled 'The data black hole at the center of AI' explores the concept of the vast amounts of data used to train AI models. It discusses how this volume of data exceeds human experience, creating a 'black hole' effect at the core of AI development. The episode may be of interest to listeners wanting to understand the scale and implications of data in AI training.
summary.en: The YouTube video titled 'The data black hole at the center of AI' explores the concept of the vast amounts of data used to train AI models. It discusses how this volume of data exceeds human experience, creating a 'black hole' effect at the core of AI development. The episode may be of interest to listeners wanting to understand the scale and implications of data in AI training.
summary.zh: 这段YouTube视频探讨训练AI模型所用的海量数据，指出其规模超越人类经验，形成AI发展的“黑洞”效应。适合想了解AI训练中数据规模及影响的观众。
summary.es: El video de YouTube explora el concepto de las enormes cantidades de datos usadas para entrenar modelos de IA, discutiendo cómo este volumen excede la experiencia humana, creando un efecto de 'agujero negro' en el núcleo del desarrollo de la IA. Interesante para quienes quieran comprender la escala e implicaciones del uso de datos en el entrenamiento de IA.

The episode promises insights into Machiavelli's historical context and ideas, making it potentially valuable for those interested in philosophy and history.

summary.original: This episode from AI & I features GitHub's COO explaining why AI has not replaced developers despite the exponential growth in code commits on GitHub. The conversation may be worth listening to for insights into the interaction between AI and software development work.
summary.en: This episode from AI & I features GitHub's COO explaining why AI has not replaced developers despite the exponential growth in code commits on GitHub. The conversation may be worth listening to for insights into the interaction between AI and software development work.

title.es: Simulando humanos a gran escala: Joon Sung Park de Simile

summary.original: This episode of Training Data features Joon Sung Park from Simile discussing the challenges of creating superintelligent models that excel at objective problems but struggle to behave like actual humans. The conversation likely explores advances in AI focused on simulating human behavior at scale, an important topic for AI development. It may appeal to listeners interested in artificial intelligence and the nuances of making machines more human-like beyond just problem-solving capabilities.
summary.en: This episode of Training Data features Joon Sung Park from Simile discussing the challenges of creating superintelligent models that excel at objective problems but struggle to behave like actual humans. It may appeal to listeners interested in artificial intelligence and the nuances of making machines more human-like beyond just problem-solving capabilities.

summary.es: En este episodio de Training Data, Joon Sung Park de Simile habla sobre los desafíos de crear modelos superinteligentes que sobresalen en problemas objetivos pero tienen dificultades para comportarse como humanos reales. La conversación explora avances en IA focalizados en simular el comportamiento humano a gran escala, tema relevante para interesados en IA y humanización de máquinas.

title.original: Red-Teaming after Mythos — Zico Kolter & Matt Fredrikson, Gray Swan
title.en: Red-Teaming after Mythos — Zico Kolter & Matt Fredrikson, Gray Swan
title.zh: Mythos之后的红队测试 — Zico Kolter & Matt Fredrikson, Gray Swan
title.es: Red-Teaming después de Mythos — Zico Kolter & Matt Fredrikson, Gray Swan

summary.original: This Latent Space podcast episode features Zico Kolter, an OpenAI board member, and Matt Fredrikson, CEO of Gray Swan, discussing AI security. They explain why AI security differs from traditional cybersecurity, focusing on vulnerabilities like prompt injection and the emerging field of automated red teaming. The episode delves into AI robustness, agent vulnerabilities, and the potential future of AI security including AI-driven attack and defense mechanisms. It's valuable for listeners interested in advanced AI safety and security topics.
summary.en: This Latent Space podcast episode features Zico Kolter, an OpenAI board member, and Matt Fredrikson, CEO of Gray Swan, discussing AI security. They explain why AI security differs from traditional cybersecurity, focusing on vulnerabilities like prompt injection and the emerging field of automated red teaming. The episode delves into AI robustness, agent vulnerabilities, and the potential future of AI security including AI-driven attack and defense mechanisms. It's valuable for listeners interested in advanced AI safety and security topics.
summary.zh: Latent Space播客中，OpenAI董事Zico Kolter与Gray Swan CEO Matt Fredrikson讨论AI安全，解释其与传统网络安全不同，聚焦提示注入、自动红队测试、AI鲁棒性及未来AI安全发展。内容适合关注先进AI安全与防护的听众。
summary.es: En este episodio de Latent Space, Zico Kolter (miembro del consejo de OpenAI) y Matt Fredrikson (CEO de Gray Swan) discuten la seguridad en IA, diferenciándola de la ciberseguridad tradicional, enfocándose en vulnerabilidades como la inyección de prompts y el red-teaming automatizado, explorando la robustez de IA y el futuro de la seguridad IA, valioso para interesados en seguridad avanzada de IA.

title.original: #397 ‒ Endometriosis and adenomyosis: diagnosis, fertility, reproductive aging, and emerging treatments | Renato Tomioka, M.D., Ph.D.
title.en: #397 ‒ Endometriosis and adenomyosis: diagnosis, fertility, reproductive aging, and emerging treatments | Renato Tomioka, M.D., Ph.D.
title.zh: #397 ‒ 子宫内膜异位症与子宫腺肌症：诊断、生育力、女性生育衰老及新兴治疗 | Renato Tomioka博士
title.es: #397 ‒ Endometriosis y adenomiosis: diagnóstico, fertilidad, envejecimiento reproductivo y tratamientos emergentes | Renato Tomioka, M.D., Ph.D.

summary.original: Episode #397 of The Peter Attia Drive features Dr. Renato Tomioka, a specialist in reproductive medicine and gynecologic surgery, discussing the diagnosis and treatment of endometriosis and adenomyosis. The episode covers how these conditions are often undiagnosed despite affecting millions of women worldwide, advancements in diagnostic imaging, decision-making between hormonal therapy and surgery, fertility preservation strategies, the impact of female age on fertility, common mistakes in treatment, and emerging therapies. Dr. Tomioka highlights the benefits of earlier diagnosis in reducing disease burden, offering a detailed and evidence-informed exploration of women's reproductive health.
summary.en: Episode #397 of The Peter Attia Drive features Dr. Renato Tomioka, a specialist in reproductive medicine and gynecologic surgery, discussing the diagnosis and treatment of endometriosis and adenomyosis. The episode covers how these conditions are often undiagnosed despite affecting millions of women worldwide, advancements in diagnostic imaging, decision-making between hormonal therapy and surgery, fertility preservation strategies, the impact of female age on fertility, common mistakes in treatment, and emerging therapies. Dr. Tomioka highlights the benefits of earlier diagnosis in reducing disease burden, offering a detailed and evidence-informed exploration of women's reproductive health.
summary.zh: 《The Peter Attia Drive》第397集，生殖医学及妇科手术专家Renato Tomioka博士讨论子宫内膜异位症与子宫腺肌症的诊断与治疗，涵盖诊断成像进展、生育力保护、年龄影响、常见治疗误区及新疗法，强调早期诊断降低疾病负担，对女性生殖健康有深入且有证据支持的讲解。
summary.es: En el episodio #397 de The Peter Attia Drive, el Dr. Renato Tomioka, especialista en medicina reproductiva y cirugía ginecológica, aborda el diagnóstico y tratamiento de la endometriosis y adenomiosis, incluyendo avances en imagenología, decisiones entre terapias hormonales y cirugía, estrategias de preservación de fertilidad, impacto de la edad, errores comunes y terapias emergentes, destacando los beneficios del diagnóstico temprano para reducir la carga de la enfermedad, ofreciendo una exploración detallada y basada en evidencia sobre salud reproductiva femenina.

title.original: The data black hole at the center of AI
title.en: The data black hole at the center of AI
title.zh: 人工智能中心的数据黑洞
title.es: El agujero negro de datos en el centro de la IA

summary.original: Dwarkesh Patel's episode examines the central challenge in AI development: the immense data requirements behind AI capabilities, described metaphorically as a "massive black hole of data". The discussion highlights that progress in AI largely stems from more and better data and increased compute, rather than improvements in sample efficiency. Patel emphasizes the extensive human expert input required to generate training data across specialized fields and compares the enormous token volumes AI models train on versus human learning. The episode suggests that despite impressive AI performance, fundamental inefficiencies in learning persist.
summary.en: Dwarkesh Patel's episode examines the central challenge in AI development: the immense data requirements behind AI capabilities, described metaphorically as a "massive black hole of data". The discussion highlights that progress in AI largely stems from more and better data and increased compute, rather than improvements in sample efficiency. Patel emphasizes the extensive human expert input required to generate training data across specialized fields and compares the enormous token volumes AI models train on versus human learning. The episode suggests that despite impressive AI performance, fundamental inefficiencies in learning persist.
summary.zh: Dwarkesh Patel在本集探讨AI发展的核心挑战：巨量数据需求，被比喻为“庞大的数据黑洞”。讨论强调AI进展主要依赖更多更好的数据和计算资源，而非样本效率提升。Patel指出生成训练数据需大量领域专家参与，并对比了AI模型训练的巨大数据量与人类学习。节目指出尽管AI表现出色，学习中仍存在根本性低效。
summary.es: El episodio de Dwarkesh Patel examina el desafío central en el desarrollo de IA: los inmensos requerimientos de datos, descritos como un "enorme agujero negro de datos". La discusión destaca que el progreso en IA proviene principalmente de más y mejores datos y mayor potencia de cómputo, no de mejoras en la eficiencia de muestras. Patel enfatiza la extensa participación de expertos humanos para generar datos de entrenamiento en campos especializados y compara los enormes volúmenes de tokens usados por modelos de IA frente al aprendizaje humano. El episodio sugiere que, pese al rendimiento impresionante, persisten ineficiencias fundamentales en el aprendizaje.

title.original: The Professor of Outputmaxxing — Anjney Midha, AMP
title.en: The Professor of Outputmaxxing — Anjney Midha, AMP
title.zh: 输出最大化教授——Anjney Midha，AMP
title.es: El Profesor de Outputmaxxing — Anjney Midha, AMP

summary.original: This Latent Space Podcast episode features Anjney Midha, founder of AMP, exploring the complexities of AI compute efficiency and the scaling challenges in frontier AI labs. He discusses why simply acquiring more GPUs doesn't guarantee better models and the importance of system-level improvements such as scheduling, networking, and utilization. Anjney shares insights into AMP's vision for a compute grid that optimizes FLOP usage, the limitations of current AI infrastructure, and the cultural and leadership aspects influencing AI research organizations. His background spans work at major tech companies and investing in AI startups.
summary.en: This Latent Space Podcast episode features Anjney Midha, founder of AMP, exploring the complexities of AI compute efficiency and the scaling challenges in frontier AI labs. He discusses why simply acquiring more GPUs doesn't guarantee better models and the importance of system-level improvements such as scheduling, networking, and utilization. Anjney shares insights into AMP's vision for a compute grid that optimizes FLOP usage, the limitations of current AI infrastructure, and the cultural and leadership aspects influencing AI research organizations. His background spans work at major tech companies and investing in AI startups.
summary.zh: Latent Space播客邀请AMP创始人Anjney Midha，探讨AI算力效率复杂性及前沿AI实验室的扩展挑战。他讨论为何单纯增加GPU数量不保证模型提升，强调系统级改进如调度、网络和利用率的重要性。Anjney介绍AMP关于算力网格优化FLOP使用的愿景，当前AI基础设施限制，以及影响AI研究机构的文化与领导力。他曾在顶级科技公司工作并投资AI初创企业。
summary.es: En este episodio del Latent Space Podcast, Anjney Midha, fundador de AMP, explora las complejidades de la eficiencia del cómputo en IA y los desafíos de escalabilidad en laboratorios de IA de frontera. Discute por qué simplemente adquirir más GPUs no garantiza mejores modelos y la importancia de mejoras a nivel de sistema como programación, redes y utilización. Anjney comparte la visión de AMP para una red de cómputo que optimice el uso de FLOPs, las limitaciones de la infraestructura actual y los aspectos culturales y de liderazgo que influyen en las organizaciones de investigación en IA. Su experiencia incluye trabajo en grandes empresas tecnológicas e inversión en startups de IA.

title.original: Re-engineering the Semiconductor Supply Chain with Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan
title.en: Re-engineering the Semiconductor Supply Chain with Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan
title.zh: 与Intel CEO谭立布共话半导体供应链再造
title.es: Reingeniería de la cadena de suministro de semiconductores con el CEO de Intel, Lip-Bu Tan

summary.original: The No Priors Podcast features a conversation with Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan, focusing on his efforts to lead Intel's turnaround. The discussion covers how his experience in startup culture influences his approach to driving changes in Intel's corporate culture and operational strategies. The episode explores the challenges and strategies involved in re-engineering the semiconductor supply chain within a leading technology company.
summary.en: The No Priors Podcast features a conversation with Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan, focusing on his efforts to lead Intel's turnaround. The discussion covers how his experience in startup culture influences his approach to driving changes in Intel's corporate culture and operational strategies. The episode explores the challenges and strategies involved in re-engineering the semiconductor supply chain within a leading technology company.
summary.zh: No Priors播客专访Intel CEO谭立布，聚焦其领导Intel转型的努力。讨论涵盖其创业文化经验如何影响推动Intel公司文化和运营策略变革。节目探讨在领先科技公司内重塑半导体供应链面临的挑战与策略。
summary.es: El podcast No Priors presenta una conversación con el CEO de Intel, Lip-Bu Tan, enfocándose en sus esfuerzos para liderar la transformación de Intel. La discusión cubre cómo su experiencia en cultura startup influye en su enfoque para impulsar cambios en la cultura corporativa y estrategias operativas de Intel. El episodio explora los desafíos y estrategias involucrados en reingeniería de la cadena de suministro de semiconductores dentro de una compañía tecnológica líder.

title.original: 🔬 The Self-Driving Lab — Joseph Krause, Radical AI
title.en: 🔬 The Self-Driving Lab — Joseph Krause, Radical AI
title.zh: 自动化实验室驱动创新——Joseph Krause，Radical AI
title.es: 🔬 El laboratorio autónomo — Joseph Krause, Radical AI

summary.original: On this episode of the Latent Space Podcast, Joseph Krause, founder of Radical AI, discusses accelerating materials science innovation through self-driving labs. He explains how Radical AI's automated labs, augmented by an "AI scientist," can generate and test hypotheses rapidly, speeding the materials discovery process by nearly tenfold compared to previous programs. The episode delves into challenges unique to materials science like complex manufacturing processes and supply chains, emphasizing that experimental data and lab automation form the industry's competitive moat. Joseph also addresses the role of public-private partnerships in advancing these technologies and shares Radical AI's commitment to open-sourcing key tools.
summary.en: On this episode of the Latent Space Podcast, Joseph Krause, founder of Radical AI, discusses accelerating materials science innovation through self-driving labs. He explains how Radical AI's automated labs, augmented by an "AI scientist," can generate and test hypotheses rapidly, speeding the materials discovery process by nearly tenfold compared to previous programs. The episode delves into challenges unique to materials science like complex manufacturing processes and supply chains, emphasizing that experimental data and lab automation form the industry's competitive moat. Joseph also addresses the role of public-private partnerships in advancing these technologies and shares Radical AI's commitment to open-sourcing key tools.
summary.zh: Latent Space播客中，Radical AI创始人Joseph Krause谈加速材料科学创新的自动化实验室。他解释了由“AI科学家”辅助的自动化实验室如何快速生成并测试假设，使材料发现速度相比以往提升近十倍。节目探讨材料科学中复杂制造与供应链的挑战，强调实验数据和实验室自动化构成行业竞争壁垒。Joseph还谈及公私合作推动技术发展及Radical AI开源关键工具的承诺。
summary.es: En este episodio del Latent Space Podcast, Joseph Krause, fundador de Radical AI, discute la aceleración de la innovación en ciencia de materiales mediante laboratorios autónomos. Explica cómo los laboratorios automatizados de Radical AI, aumentados por un "científico de IA", pueden generar y probar hipótesis rápidamente, acelerando el proceso de descubrimiento de materiales casi diez veces comparado con programas anteriores. El episodio aborda desafíos únicos de la ciencia de materiales como procesos de manufactura complejos y cadenas de suministro, enfatizando que los datos experimentales y la automatización de laboratorios forman la ventaja competitiva de la industria. Joseph también comenta el papel de colaboraciones público-privadas y el compromiso de Radical AI con el código abierto.

title.original: Ada Palmer – Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time
title.en: Ada Palmer – Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time
title.zh: Ada Palmer——马基雅维利是最被误解的思想家
title.es: Ada Palmer – Maquiavelo es el pensador más incomprendido de todos los tiempos

summary.original: This episode of the Dwarkesh Podcast features Ada Palmer discussing Niccolò Machiavelli, described as perhaps the most misunderstood thinker of all time. Machiavelli was a high-level diplomat for Florence who observed key rulers in Europe during turbulent times. After the Medici retook Florence and exiled him, Machiavelli dedicated his book The Prince to Lorenzo di Piero de’ Medici. The conversation explores Machiavelli's insights on power, politics, and human nature, inspired by his diplomatic experiences and historical context. Ada Palmer is also launching a new podcast about Machiavelli for deeper exploration. The episode might interest listeners curious about Renaissance politics and Machiavelli's legacy.
summary.en: This episode of the Dwarkesh Podcast features Ada Palmer discussing Niccolò Machiavelli, described as perhaps the most misunderstood thinker of all time. Machiavelli was a high-level diplomat for Florence who observed key rulers in Europe during turbulent times. After the Medici retook Florence and exiled him, Machiavelli dedicated his book The Prince to Lorenzo di Piero de’ Medici. The conversation explores Machiavelli's insights on power, politics, and human nature, inspired by his diplomatic experiences and historical context. Ada Palmer is also launching a new podcast about Machiavelli for deeper exploration. The episode might interest listeners curious about Renaissance politics and Machiavelli's legacy.
summary.zh: Dwarkesh播客本集邀请Ada Palmer谈论尼科洛·马基雅维利，称其或为史上最被误解的思想家。马基雅维利曾是佛罗伦萨高级外交官，见证欧洲动荡时期多位重要统治者。美第奇家族重新掌权后驱逐他，他将著作《君主论》献给洛伦佐·皮耶罗·德·美第奇。对话探讨马基雅维利关于权力、政治和人性的见解，及其外交经验与历史背景的启发。Ada Palmer即将推出新播客，深入解读马基雅维利。适合关注文艺复兴政治与其遗产的听众。
summary.es: Este episodio del podcast Dwarkesh presenta a Ada Palmer discutiendo a Niccolò Maquiavelo, descrito como quizá el pensador más incomprendido de todos los tiempos. Maquiavelo fue un diplomático de alto nivel en Florencia que observó a gobernantes clave en Europa durante tiempos turbulentos. Tras la reconquista de Florencia por los Medici y su exilio, dedicó su libro El Príncipe a Lorenzo di Piero de’ Medici. La conversación explora las ideas de Maquiavelo sobre poder, política y naturaleza humana, inspiradas en sus experiencias diplomáticas y contexto histórico. Ada Palmer lanzará un nuevo podcast para profundizar en Maquiavelo. El episodio interesa a quienes siguen política renacentista y su legado.

title.original: The hidden pattern behind successful products | Mark Pincus (FarmVille, Words with Friends, & more)
title.en: The hidden pattern behind successful products | Mark Pincus (FarmVille, Words with Friends, & more)
title.zh: 成功产品背后的隐藏模式 | Mark Pincus（FarmVille、《Words with Friends》等）
title.es: El patrón oculto detrás de productos exitosos | Mark Pincus (FarmVille, Words with Friends y más)

summary.original: In this episode of Lenny's Podcast, Mark Pincus, founder of Zynga, shares insights into the hidden patterns behind successful products. Known for consumer hits like FarmVille and Words With Friends, Pincus discusses his experience in creating popular digital games. This episode could interest those curious about product development, game design, and understanding factors that contribute to widespread product success.
summary.en: In this episode of Lenny's Podcast, Mark Pincus, founder of Zynga, shares insights into the hidden patterns behind successful products. Known for consumer hits like FarmVille and Words With Friends, Pincus discusses his experience in creating popular digital games. This episode could interest those curious about product development, game design, and understanding factors that contribute to widespread product success.
summary.zh: Lenny播客中，Zynga创始人Mark Pincus分享了成功产品背后的隐藏规律。作为FarmVille及《Words with Friends》等热门游戏的设计者，他谈及数字游戏开发经验，适合对产品开发、游戏设计及成功因素感兴趣的听众。
summary.es: En este episodio del podcast de Lenny, Mark Pincus, fundador de Zynga, comparte insights sobre los patrones ocultos detrás de productos exitosos. Conocido por hits como FarmVille y Words with Friends, Pincus discute su experiencia creando juegos digitales populares. Episodio recomendado para quienes se interesan en desarrollo de productos, diseño de juegos y factores de éxito.

title.original: The hidden pattern behind successful products | Mark Pincus (founder of Zynga)
title.en: The hidden pattern behind successful products | Mark Pincus (founder of Zynga)
title.zh: 成功产品背后的隐藏模式
title.es: El patrón oculto detrás de los productos exitosos

summary.original: In this episode of Lenny's Podcast, Mark Pincus, founder of Zynga, shares lessons from creating numerous successful consumer games including Words With Friends and FarmVille. With eight out of ten major launches becoming hits, Pincus discusses frameworks such as “Proven, Better, New” for product success and why less ambitious ideas can lead to greater innovation. The conversation includes his views on intuition, idea validation, and raising children in the AI era. Pincus also announces his forthcoming book, Life at the Speed of Play. This episode offers valuable insights for product builders and startup enthusiasts.
summary.en: In this episode of Lenny's Podcast, Mark Pincus, founder of Zynga, shares lessons from creating numerous successful consumer games including Words With Friends and FarmVille. With eight out of ten major launches becoming hits, Pincus discusses frameworks such as “Proven, Better, New” for product success and why less ambitious ideas can lead to greater innovation. The conversation includes his views on intuition, idea validation, and raising children in the AI era. Pincus also announces his forthcoming book, Life at the Speed of Play. This episode offers valuable insights for product builders and startup enthusiasts.
summary.zh: 本期Lenny播客邀请Zynga创始人Mark Pincus分享打造热门游戏的经验，探讨“经过验证、更好、新颖”的产品成功框架，并讨论直觉、验证创意及AI时代育儿观点。Pincus还介绍即将出版的新书《Life at the Speed of Play》。
summary.es: En este episodio del Podcast de Lenny, Mark Pincus, fundador de Zynga, comparte lecciones sobre juegos exitosos como Words With Friends y FarmVille, discute el marco “Probado, Mejor, Nuevo” para el éxito de productos y sus puntos de vista sobre intuición, validación de ideas y crianza en la era de la IA. Anuncia su próximo libro Life at the Speed of Play.

title.original: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
title.en: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
title.zh: Sarah Paine：为何普京和习近平逃不过地理限制
title.es: Sarah Paine - Por qué Putin y Xi no pueden escapar de la geografía

summary.original: Military historian Sarah Paine presents a lecture on the geopolitical framework shaping global power over two millennia, focusing on why leaders like Putin and Xi cannot escape geographic constraints. The episode delves into historical and strategic geography influencing current geopolitics. It may attract listeners keen on understanding the lasting impact of geography on international relations and leadership decisions.
summary.en: Military historian Sarah Paine presents a lecture on the geopolitical framework shaping global power over two millennia, focusing on why leaders like Putin and Xi cannot escape geographic constraints. The episode delves into historical and strategic geography influencing current geopolitics. It may attract listeners keen on understanding the lasting impact of geography on international relations and leadership decisions.
summary.zh: 军事历史学家Sarah Paine讲解了塑造全球权力两千年的地缘政治框架，并重点分析为何领导人如普京和习近平无法摆脱地理限制。内容涵盖影响当代地缘政治的历史与战略地理，适合关注国际关系与领导决策地理影响的听众。
summary.es: La historiadora militar Sarah Paine presenta una conferencia sobre el marco geopolítico que ha moldeado el poder global durante dos milenios, enfocándose en por qué líderes como Putin y Xi no pueden escapar de las limitaciones geográficas. Se profundiza en la geografía histórica y estratégica que influye en la geopolítica actual, ideal para quienes desean entender el impacto duradero de la geografía en relaciones internacionales y liderazgo.

title.original: Tony Fadell: How to build real taste (and why AI makes it matter more)
title.en: Tony Fadell: How to build real taste (and why AI makes it matter more)
title.zh: Tony Fadell：如何培养真正的品味（以及为什么AI让这更重要）
title.es: Tony Fadell: Cómo desarrollar un gusto real (y por qué la IA lo hace más relevante)

summary.original: Tony Fadell appears on Lenny's Podcast to discuss how to develop real taste in design and why this skill becomes increasingly important in the age of AI. Fadell's background includes creating the iPod, co-creating the iPhone, founding Nest, and holding over 300 patents. The episode offers valuable perspectives on design, innovation, and the intersection of technology and aesthetics for listeners interested in product development and AI's influence on creativity.
summary.en: Tony Fadell appears on Lenny's Podcast to discuss how to develop real taste in design and why this skill becomes increasingly important in the age of AI. Fadell's background includes creating the iPod, co-creating the iPhone, founding Nest, and holding over 300 patents. The episode offers valuable perspectives on design, innovation, and the intersection of technology and aesthetics for listeners interested in product development and AI's influence on creativity.
summary.zh: Tony Fadell在Lenny播客中谈论如何在设计中培养真正的品味，以及为什么在人工智能时代这项技能变得更加重要。作为iPod创造者、iPhone联合创造者、Nest创始人及300多项专利持有者，Fadell分享了对设计、创新及科技与美学交汇的深刻见解，适合对产品开发和AI影响创意感兴趣的听众。
summary.es: Tony Fadell aparece en el podcast de Lenny para discutir cómo desarrollar un gusto real en el diseño y por qué esta habilidad es cada vez más importante en la era de la IA. Con experiencia creando el iPod, co-creando el iPhone, fundando Nest y con más de 300 patentes, Fadell ofrece perspectivas valiosas sobre diseño, innovación y la intersección entre tecnología y estética para quienes se interesan en el desarrollo de productos y la influencia de la IA en la creatividad.

title.original: Anthropic Expert: Rethink What's Possible With Fable 5
title.en: Anthropic Expert: Rethink What's Possible With Fable 5
title.zh: Anthropic专家：重新思考Fable 5的可能性
title.es: Experto de Anthropic: Reconsidera lo posible con Fable 5

Mike Krieger, cofounder of Instagram, is mentioned as being at the forefront of AI development post his experience building impactful consumer applications. The episode encourages listeners to rethink possibilities with AI through exploration of this technology. It may appeal to those interested in artificial intelligence, innovative AI platforms, and technology at the cutting edge of AI development.
summary.en: This episode from AI & I YouTube features an expert from Anthropic discussing Fable 5, an AI-native platform. Mike Krieger, cofounder of Instagram, is mentioned as being at the forefront of AI development post his experience building impactful consumer applications. The episode encourages listeners to rethink possibilities with AI through exploration of this technology. It may appeal to those interested in artificial intelligence, innovative AI platforms, and technology at the cutting edge of AI development.
